metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `validate_checks.py` to the Scope and add an AC item to exempt `idea`-status tickets from `check_orphaned_tasks` (e.g. skip tickets whose status is `idea`, mirroring the `closed` skip), and confirm the `--validate exemptions` note in CLAUDE.md describes the actual code behavior.
findings.finding: validate_checks.py check_orphaned_tasks will flag idea-status tickets with no parent as orphaned tasks (WARNING, possibly MAJOR for clusters), contradicting the intended semantics of idea as a valid captured-but-undesigned status. The check skips only closed tickets (`status == 'closed'`), so an idea ticket without a parent epic will surface as an orphan warning. The plan mentions documenting 'validate exemptions' in CLAUDE.md but does not add validate_checks.py to Scope, does not include an AC for the code fix, and does not describe what the exemption actually is.
findings.location: Scope section (file list); src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py check_orphaned_tasks; AC item for CLAUDE.md validate exemptions
findings.suggested_fix: In check_orphaned_tasks, extend the skip condition to also exclude idea-status tickets: change `if issue.get('status', 'open') == 'closed': continue` to `if issue.get('status', 'open') in ('closed', 'idea'): continue`. Add src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py to the Scope section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py` to Scope, specify which validate checks should exempt `idea`-status tickets (e.g. `check_orphaned_tasks` skipping `idea` the same way it skips `closed`), and add a corresponding AC item: '`rebar validate` does not emit orphan/empty-epic warnings for `idea`-status tickets (test-verified)'.
findings.finding: The plan mentions 'validate exemptions' in the CLAUDE.md documentation AC item but never specifies what those exemptions are, and `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py` is absent from the Scope. As code-verified, `check_orphaned_tasks` skips only `closed` tickets; an `idea` ticket without a parent epic would generate false-positive WARNING/MAJOR orphaned-task findings under `rebar validate`. The plan's promise to document 'validate exemptions' is hollow without specifying and implementing them.
findings.location: ## What (bullet: 'Keep idea OUT of the open-ish sets') / ## Scope / AC: 'The committed CLAUDE.md documents the idea status and its lifecycle (transitions, close-bypass, validate exemptions, Jira prerequisite)'
findings.suggested_fix: In `validate_checks.py`, in `check_orphaned_tasks`, change the status exclusion from `== 'closed'` to `in ('closed', 'idea')` (or a shared `_NON_DISPATCH_STATUSES` set). Add to Scope and add an AC item.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the hardcoded status list in the `_validate_status` error string (transition.py line 166) to include `idea` alongside the existing four statuses, so the error message stays consistent with `_VALID_STATUSES`.
findings.finding: The transition.py hardcoded error message string at line 166 (`"Must be one of: open, in_progress, closed, blocked"`) is an unenumerated edit target in the named file. After adding `idea` to `_VALID_STATUSES`, this string will be stale — it will no longer list all valid statuses, which misleads users who type an actual invalid status after `idea` ships.
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 166 to: `f"Error: invalid {label} '{value}'. Must be one of: open, in_progress, closed, blocked, idea"`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly decide and state in the Scope section whether `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py` needs to be updated to exempt `idea`-status tickets from orphan/validate checks (or confirm by omission that surfacing them as orphans is the intended behavior).
findings.finding: The plan scopes `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py` out entirely, but the AC promises 'validate exemptions' documented in CLAUDE.md — implying the implementer must understand how `validate` treats `idea`-status tickets. At minimum `check_orphaned_tasks` treats any non-`closed` ticket as a candidate orphan warning, so every `idea` ticket that has no parent epic will surface as a WARNING (or MAJOR cluster), which may be undesired behavior that needs either a code change or deliberate documentation.
findings.location: Scope section (no entry for validate_checks.py); AC line 'The committed CLAUDE.md documents the idea status and its lifecycle (transitions, close-bypass, validate exemptions, Jira prerequisite)'; src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py line 40-41

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the integrated-suite AC: either move it to the last sibling story's AC list (the one that merges after all others) or add a note that it is a deferred/post-integration gate, not a pre-merge gate for this foundational story.
findings.finding: The AC 'The full test suite passes with all five sibling changes integrated (this foundational story owns the integrated-suite check)' requires all sibling stories to be merged before this AC can pass, but the Prerequisite/Why section explicitly states 'this story should land first' before the four dependent child stories. These two directives are mutually exclusive: landing first means the siblings aren't integrated yet, so the integrated-suite AC cannot be satisfied at merge time.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last integration AC; Why section — 'this story should land first'
findings.suggested_fix: Move the integrated-suite AC to the last sibling story (e.g. story 2918-24c8-4c2a-4e10 or whichever merges last) and replace it here with 'The unit/integration tests added in this story pass in isolation (sibling integration verified in final child story).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `idea → closed` bypass: either (a) add an AC item `transition <id> idea closed` succeeds with exit 0 (confirming it is a plain transition with no special bypass in this story), or (b) specify which gate is bypassed, add a scope entry for the bypass implementation, and add a test-verified AC item.
findings.finding: The CLAUDE.md documentation AC item references 'the `idea → closed` reject/drop bypass' as lifecycle content to document, but no AC item tests `transition <id> idea closed` succeeds, and no scope item specifies what 'bypass' behavior (if any) applies. It is ambiguous whether `idea → closed` is (a) a plain transition that works automatically once `idea` is in `_VALID_STATUSES`, (b) a gated transition with special close-gate bypass, or (c) a future-story feature being pre-documented. An executor cannot implement or test what isn't specified.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — 'CLAUDE.md documents the `idea` status and its lifecycle (transitions, close-bypass, validate exemptions, Jira prerequisite)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'test-verified' to ACs 3–5 and include a Scope step to write regression tests for the `open→idea`, `idea→open`, and `idea→in_progress` transition edges (mirroring the 'test-verified' pattern used for ready/next-batch/search).
findings.finding: The three transition ACs (3–5) state 'succeeds with exit 0' but neither the Scope section nor the AC list mentions writing regression tests for these new edges; unlike ACs 8–10 they are not marked 'test-verified,' leaving the proving command as an ephemeral manual invocation rather than a durable test artifact.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria lines: 'transition <id> open idea succeeds with exit 0', 'transition <id> idea open succeeds with exit 0', 'transition <id> idea in_progress succeeds with exit 0'; Scope: src/rebar/_commands/transition.py
findings.suggested_fix: Append '(test-verified)' to each of ACs 3–5 and add a bullet under Scope: 'Add pytest parametrized cases for the `open→idea`, `idea→open`, and `idea→in_progress` edges in the existing transition test module.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether `idea → in_progress` transitions are exempt from the plan-review gate (and if so, add `idea`-status to the gate's exemption logic in `gates.py`/`transition_compute`), or whether `--force` is always required for this edge.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `transition <id> idea in_progress` succeeds with exit 0 (AC item), but `transition_compute` unconditionally runs the plan-review precheck gate for ANY transition whose `target_status == 'in_progress'` (transition.py lines 256-264: `if cascade and target_status == 'in_progress': gates.plan_review_precheck(...)`). The plan's Scope and What sections make no mention of gate-exempting `idea`-status tickets for this edge, so unless `idea` is added to the gate's exempt set (currently only `bug`/`session_log` are exempt per CLAUDE.md), the AC `transition <id> idea in_progress` will be blocked by a missing/stale attestation when the plan-review gate is enabled.
findings.location: AC: `transition <id> idea in_progress` succeeds with exit 0 / Scope section / src/rebar/_commands/transition.py lines 256-264

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (by reading `rebar.reducer.apply_ticket_filters`) that the status filter is a string-equality match against the ticket's `status` field, not a membership check against a hardcoded status set, so `idea` passes through without additional changes to the reducer.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Add `idea` to the accepted `list --status` filter set' and lists `reads_cli.py` in Scope, but the `_VALID_LIST_STATUSES` frozenset in reads_cli.py (lines 71-73) is defined with a comment stating it covers 'the four lifecycle states plus archived/deleted tombstones and the reducer's error/fsck_needed sentinels.' Adding `idea` to the schema enum and `_VALID_LIST_STATUSES` is necessary but there is also an identical hardcoded status membership check in the `_cmd_list` error message on line 296 (`Valid statuses: {', '.join(sorted(_VALID_LIST_STATUSES))}`). The plan does not call out that this error-message string is auto-derived from the frozenset (it is), so this is actually fine — but the plan should confirm that the reducer's `apply_ticket_filters` (called from `list_states`) also passes the new status value through, since it is the actual filter engine.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `idea`-status tickets are excluded from the Jira reconciler's outbound differ (or add `idea` to `excluded_statuses` / the status-mapping kill-switch path) so that this story can land safely before the Jira `IDEA` mapping sibling story without aborting reconcile passes.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the Jira 'IDEA' mapping is a prerequisite handled by a sibling story, but does not verify that `idea`-status tickets are safe in the interim. The existing reconciler's `preflight_status_mapping` in `reconcile.py` raises `StatusMappingError` when any outbound update mutation references a local status absent from `config.local_to_jira_status`, and `run_differs.py:324` hardcodes `excluded_statuses={"archived", "deleted"}` — `idea` is not in that set. An `idea`-status `story`/`task` ticket flowing through the outbound differ's mutation loop (e.g. on a title/description edit) would trigger the preflight and abort the entire reconcile pass until the Jira sibling story lands.
findings.location: ## What — 'Document the `idea` status AND its lifecycle' / 'Jira `IDEA` mapping prerequisite'; Scope does not include reconciler files
findings.suggested_fix: Add `idea` to `config.local_to_jira_status` as a no-op mapping (e.g. `"idea": "To Do"` as a temporary stand-in, or a placeholder `"IDEA"` that Jira ignores), OR add a status-based exclusion alongside the type-based `EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES` so `idea`-status tickets are skipped by the outbound differ the same way `archived`/`deleted` tickets are. The cleanest safe-harbor for this foundational story is to add `"idea"` to the `excluded_statuses` set passed in `run_differs.py:324` (or to `config.py` so it is config-driven), pending the Jira sibling landing.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
